mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 PAYEMS at 135474 thousand shows ongoing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.50% while remaining below 2.0%, the Fed is in a measured tightening cycle that signals confidence in economic recovery but does not yet constrain equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts positive returns as markets interpret gradual rate increases as validation of growth, not recessionary policy.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.50% and remains below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 FEDFUNDS at 0.15% is below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances when trigger is active, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) move together in demand-driven cycles. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, it signals broad-based consumer and construction demand that supports equity earnings. This interaction-driven mechanism requires joint positive momentum in both proxies to activate the signal.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both show positive 3-month momentum
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 HOUST at 1036 thousand, RSAFS at 419027 million
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20d returns in > 58% of instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines of more than 1.5% over three months signal supply-side weakness or demand destruction that compresses profit margins and reduces forward equity returns. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates when production momentum turns negative, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines > 1.5% fail to produce negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) increases of more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month signal rapid labor market deterioration that triggers flight-to-safety equity selling and reduces forward returns. This regime-conditional mechanism is most powerful in late-cycle expansions where labor market surprises carry high information content about recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 UNRATE at 7.5% reflects ongoing post-crisis normalization
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion where labor surprises signal recession risk
failure_condition: Monthly UNRATE increases > 0.3pp fail to produce negative 20d returns in > 62% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, creating a negative real rate environment that erodes bond returns and forces equity risk premium compression. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both conditions to activate, predicting positive equity returns as investors rotate into stocks seeking real returns.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Joint condition (inflation > 2.5% YoY and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%) fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.07 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized rate, signaling robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when GDP growth momentum is above trend.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 GDPC1 at 13750.1 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or above-trend GDP growth fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline simultaneously over a 3-month period, signaling synchronized supply and labor weakness that indicates recession onset. This interaction-driven mechanism requires joint deterioration in both series to activate, predicting negative S&P 500 returns as markets price in earnings contraction.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both show negative 3-month momentum
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 both series show post-crisis recovery momentum
failure_condition: Joint negative momentum in INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to produce negative 20d returns in > 65% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
